attemptDelta: Attempt 2 changed only style-level details vs attempt 1: (1) heading 'Accessing role names from query results' → imperative 'Access role names from query results' per INV-style-002, (2) added bold title '**Lazy loading of role data**' inside note callout per INV-callout-002. Neither change affects persona targeting, tone, depth, classification, or taxonomy. All persona findings from attempt 1 carry forward unchanged.

personaChecks.evidence: Front matter declares `persona: developer`. The entire page is a developer API reference — method signatures, query builder parameterization, interface implementations, and C# code examples. No admin, business, or architect audiences are served. Consistent with all sibling pages in content-item-api cluster (content-item-query-api.md, content-item-database-structure.md, content-item-asset-upload-api.md all target `developer`). Tag is correct and sufficient.

Uses exact API terminology throughout: `ContentItemQueryBuilder`, `IContentQueryDataContainer`, `ContentItemRolesResultProcessor`, `ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames`, `IReadOnlyCollection<string>`. Assumes SDK familiarity — references subquery parameterization, model binding, and result selectors without introductory explanation. Developer terms like 'lazy loading', 'post-processing step', 'empty collection' are used naturally. Aligns with INV-persona-003.

The TASK-009 content (lines 1024–1126, 'Query result container properties' and 'ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames') maintains developer-grade precision. Property table documents Type, Default value, and Defined in — standard API reference structure. Lazy loading note (line 1080–1083) uses precise language: 'Member role data is only fetched from the database when at least one content item in the query result has `ContentItemIsSecured` set to *true*.' Code example (lines 1094–1126) demonstrates full pattern: builder → executor options → result mapping lambda → post-query role inspection. Attempt 2 heading change ('Access role names from query results') uses imperative mood — still developer-appropriate and clearer as a task-oriented subheading.

Appropriate reference depth for developer audience. Not too shallow — documents type, default, populating mechanism, lazy loading behavior, linked item resolution, and provides a complete code example. Not too deep — does not explain C# generics, LINQ, or framework internals. The new section provides the lookup information developers need: what the property returns, when it's populated, performance characteristics (lazy loading), and how to use it in code.

No content targets admin, business, or architect personas. No GUI procedures (admin), no benefit/outcome language (business), no architecture trade-off discussions (architect). Security content stays at the code level — how to access role names programmatically — not how to configure member roles in the admin UI.

classificationCheck.evidence: Page is structured as API reference: organized by method/class with signatures, parameters, return types, behavioral notes, and code examples. Title follows 'Reference - Content item query' pattern per INV-structure-019. The new ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames section adds a property reference entry (type table, default, definition source, behavior) — pure reference content. No tutorial flow or goal-oriented procedures.

taxonomyChecks.evidence: Valid persona value per project persona definitions.
Identifier present and unchanged from existing page.
License tier present, set to 1 (standard) — appropriate for _documentation collection.
Redirect from short-link present and matches identifier.

consistencyCheck.notes: All content-item-api cluster pages target `developer` persona. Sibling reference page (reference-content-retriever-api.md) also targets developer+architect, but the architect addition there is justified by its system-level scope. This page's developer-only targeting is consistent with its narrower API reference focus.

invariantResults.evidence: Business user tone rules — page does not target business users.
Business user team assumptions — page does not target business users.

Developer terminology used throughout: lazy loading, query executor, model binding, result selector, post-processing. Related steps combined in code example (build query + set options + execute + inspect results). Matches rule: 'can combine 2 related easy steps. Use developer terminology appropriately.'

Vocabulary is strictly developer-oriented. 'Member roles' and 'secured items' are used in their API/code context with cross-references to ContentAccessSettings reference page for the broader model. No unexplained business/marketing terms.

Reference page uses direct, precise tone appropriate for lookup material. Uses 'you' form in introductory sentences ('When you run a content item query...') maintaining approachable tone without sacrificing precision.
Business user guide materials — page is a developer API reference.

Uses 'member' correctly throughout — refers to registered visitors with member roles, not admin users or generic visitors. 'Member role data', 'member roles required for access' — consistent with the defined user types.
Title follows 'Reference - Content item query' pattern as required for reference pages.

All API members in TASK-009 section backtick-formatted: `ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames`, `IContentQueryDataContainer`, `ContentItemRolesResultProcessor`, `ContentItemIsSecured`, `IncludeSecuredItems`, `IReadOnlyCollection<string>`, `WithLinkedItems`.
Code example in 'Access role names from query results' broken into logical sections with comments: query building, execution options, result mapping lambda, and post-query role checking loop.
